figure.title: Dual Operand 4-Byte Atomic Operation Data Packing
figure.purpose: Shows the layout for a dual-operand (Op1/Op2) 4-byte Atomic operation and its returned data.

technical_summary: Operands are 4 bytes each, taking one Doubleword.
Byte Enables are 0xF for each active 4-byte operand element.
Op1 Elements (E0, E1, E2, E3) are mapped to DW0, DW4, DW6, DW7.
Op2 Elements (E0, E1, E2, E3) are mapped to DW8, DW12, DW14, DW15.
Returned Data (Dat E0, Dat E1, Dat E2, Dat E3) for AtomicR is returned in the DW locations of Op1.

packet_layout.description: Originator Data channel carrying 4-byte Op1 and Op2 operands
Read Response Data channel carrying 4-byte returned values in Op1 locations

critical_facts: In dual-operand atomic requests, Op2 data is typically shifted to higher doublewords, while Op1 data resides in the lower doublewords, and return data maps to Op1's doublewords.
